command: $ErrorActionPreference='Stop'; $pscp='pscp.exe'; $log="$env:ProgramData\NetAdmin\logs\T1597.log"; $host='sftp.spycloud.com'; $user=$env:SPYCLOUD_USER; $key=$env:SPYCLOUD_PPK; $hostkey=$env:SPYCLOUD_HOSTKEY; $remote='/customer_exports/daily/credential-exposures-20250930.csv.gz'; $dest='C:\Intel\SpyCloud\credential-exposures-20250930.csv.gz'; New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path (Split-Path $dest) -Force | Out-Null; try { & $pscp -scp -batch -q -C -P 22 -i $key -hostkey $hostkey "$user@$host:$remote" "$dest"; if($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0){ throw "pscp exit code $LASTEXITCODE" }; $hash=(Get-FileHash -Algorithm SHA256 -Path $dest).Hash; $status=[pscustomobject]@{ technique='T1597'; source=$host; remote_path=$remote; local_path=$dest; sha256=$hash; success=$true; ts=(Get-Date).ToString('o') } } catch { $status=[pscustomobject]@{ technique='T1597'; source=$host; remote_path=$remote; local_path=$dest; success=$false; error=$_.Exception.Message; ts=(Get-Date).ToString('o') } } finally { $json=$status|ConvertTo-Json -Compress; New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path (Split-Path $log) -Force | Out-Null; Add-Content -Path $log -Value $json; Write-Output $json }
explanation: Retrieves a closed-source breach exposure export from SpyCloud over SCP with key-based authentication, suitable for private credential exposure assessment.
rationale_steps: Use authenticated access to a commercial data broker feed (closed source).
Download a specific daily export quietly to avoid excessive log noise.
Verify file integrity and log a compact JSON status for traceability.
Avoids any user targeting or delivery activity, focusing strictly on data collection.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Well-formed PowerShell with balanced try/catch/finally, correct quoting, valid PSCP flags, and proper hashtable and pipeline usage.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It retrieves a closed-source SpyCloud export with key-based auth, hashes and logs it—aligned with T1597—though forcing SCP against an sftp.* host may fail if the server is SFTP-only.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell command is well-formed with balanced quotes, braces, and parentheses, proper semicolon separators, valid try/catch/finally blocks, and correct cmdlet syntax throughout.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements T1597 by using pscp.exe to retrieve a credential exposure export from SpyCloud (a legitimate closed-source threat intelligence vendor) over SCP with key-based authentication, storing credentials in environment variables, computing a SHA256 hash for integrity verification, and logging structured JSON output — all operationally plausible for querying a paid closed-source intelligence feed.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is a perfectly formed PowerShell script using correct syntax for variables, try-catch-finally blocks, external process execution, and object manipulation.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements the technique by automating the retrieval of a non-public data export from a known closed-source intelligence provider (SpyCloud) using industry-standard tools and authentication.
